description: Full-AOI NEON benchmark targets (issue #202 legs 1+5), collapsed to the SINGLE point-pipeline release leg per issue #250 (espg-approved restructure on PR #256): o9, hive store layout, sharded, tdigest, inline read, WITH the strict AOI binary mask, all shards over the AOP_NEON.geojson box, recorded PER RELEASE. The former inline/sidecar x mask/nomask 2x2 and the flat parity arm are retired (their rows stay in full_aoi_series.parquet; figures move to Archived). The raster release leg (Sentinel-2 2025) rides its own manifest (targets_raster_neon.json) and harness. run_full_aoi_benchmark.py builds the shardmap over the AOI+temporal+cmr below from the PINNED catalog catalogs/cat_neon.parquet (2089 granules, offline, fixed across releases: the series tracks CODE change, not CMR drift; rebuild via run_full_aoi_benchmark._prefilter + Catalog.to_geoparquet). The whole-AOI build fans to 4 o9 shards. Dispatched with profile=True so the worker phase split (read/index/aggregate/write) lands in the series.

dispatch.note: Default dispatch target; the workflow overrides region/function from the shared BENCHMARK_* repo vars (the same ones the per-merge run uses). No account is pinned -- the release run reuses the per-merge benchmark role, and run_benchmark.py asserts no account either. A fork may add an optional expect_account here to make the harness assert the caller account before billing (it no-ops when absent).

full_aoi_neon_o9_hive_mask._comment: The whole point-pipeline release leg (issue #250 collapse): the per-merge hive config plus the strict AOI binary mask (issue #101), every NEON shard. Record-only like the rest of the release leg (espg ruling on PR #242: a release is never blocked on it).
